summary: No reduced-precision reduction is adopted. The one candidate reduction evaluated (MoE experts bf16 -> bfloat8_b) FAILED the diffusion-decision-fidelity gate, so the shipping policy stays all-bf16 weights with the fp32 decision path. bf16 is selected on FIDELITY grounds (bfp8 fits DRAM comfortably and is ~8% faster).

experts.bfp8_verdict: REJECTED — committed-argmax agreement 0.227, entropy PCC 0.631, accept IoU 0.501 vs bf16 reference

router.evidence: MoEBlock router_dtype default bf16 (moe.py); router is on the decision path, kept high precision, NOT swept

lm_head.note: produces the logits on the decision path; kept high precision, NOT swept

layer_exceptions: none — the sweep was model-uniform (all 30 layers same policy).

compute_fidelities.note: Compute fidelity was NOT swept in this stage — only expert weight dtype. LoFi/BFP4 matmul groups were not explored because the bf16->bfp8 weight reduction already fails the fidelity gate.

ccl_dtype: bfloat16 (backbone TP=4 all-reduce/all-gather via gemma4 CCL; sparse-MoE dispatch scratch bf16, cumsum fp32)
canvas_scratch_dtype: bfloat16 (per-step recomputed canvas K/V) — NOT swept (capacity unchanged)
logits_to_probability_entropy_dtype: float32 (tt/sampling.py token_entropy, gumbel_max, sample_gumbel_noise all fp32) — high-sensitivity decision path, kept fp32, explicitly validated as unchanged by the knob

capacity_impact.note: The sweep changed ONLY expert WEIGHT dtype, not KV-cache or canvas-scratch dtype, so max_model_len / context_contract capacity is unaffected. (For the record, bfp8 experts WOULD free ~5.44 GiB/chip of weight DRAM — favourable for the 256K-KV fit — but is rejected on fidelity.)

consumption_proof: Both the perf sweep and the decision-agreement harness build through checkpoint.build_tt_model_from_checkpoint_inputs -> precision_build.create_tt_model_dg, which passes Gemma4Precision({'experts': <dtype>}) into Gemma4Model. bf16 (default, no knob) loads *_dtype_BFLOAT16 expert caches at 13.268 GiB/chip; bfp8 (DG_EXPERTS_BFP8=1) loads *_dtype_BFLOAT8_B expert caches at 7.830 GiB/chip. The runtime path demonstrably consumes the selected dtype.

knob.default: OFF (bf16 experts) — no behaviour change vs pre-sweep
knob.reuse: opt-in for future work once #48291 creates decision-fidelity headroom; DO NOT enable by default
